sections.paragraph:
τοί (prop. ethical dat. of συ, L-S-J-M, s. also Denniston 537; Schwyzer I 602) enclitic particle (Hom.+; LXX; Jos., Ant. 15, 374; 16, 319; Just., A I, 4, 8 καὶ γάρ τοι) marker of emphasis on the reliability of a statement, let me tell you, surely, in our lit. only in the transition formula πέρας γέ τοι and furthermore, besides (πέρας 3) B 5:8; 10:2; 12:6; 15:6, 8; 16:3.—For μέν τοι s. μέντοι.—DELG s.v. το-1.

prop. prop. = properly

dat. dat. = dative

L-S-J-M L-S-J-M = (L-S-J) HLiddell/RScott, A Greek-English Lexicon; New Edition by HSJones/RMcKenzie—List 6

Denniston Denniston = JD., The Greek Particles—List 6

Schwyzer Schwyzer = ES., Griechische Grammatik—List 6

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1009
